title: Ceraia clavator
taxonomy: Ceraia clavator is a species of moth in the family Sphingidae. It belongs to the genus Ceraia, which consists of various species of hawk moths. The genus Ceraia is further classified within the subfamily Macroglossinae, which is a group of large hawk moths. The subfamily Macroglossinae is a part of the family Sphingidae, which is a group of moths commonly known as hawk moths. Ceraia clavator is a specific species within this complex taxonomic classification.

The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
